title: boswellia frereana
intro: Boswellia frereana is a species of tree within the Boswellia genus, commonly known as frankincense, which is a type of resinous tree sap collected from the tree's bark. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Boswellia frereana include reducing joint pain and inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and alleviating anxiety and stress. The resin is typically harvested and used in herbal preparations such as capsules, teas, and essential oils, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its therapeutic compounds.
